steps.title: Identify the pattern: claim followed by evidence
steps.content: The passage asserts "not all cascades are beneficial," then introduces a specific study as proof. The transition must signal confirmation — that the evidence supports the claim.

steps.title: Select the word expressing confirmation
steps.content:
The logical relationship is confirmation: the warbler study is concrete evidence that proves the claim that not all cascades are beneficial. The blank needs a transition word signaling that the following proves what was just stated.
  • Choice A — "Instead,": signals a substitution or alternative, so it fails because no replacement is being offered — the study supports the claim. ✘
  • Choice B — "In response,": signals a reaction to something, so it fails because the study is not a reaction to the claim about cascades — it is evidence of it. ✘
  • Choice C — "Indeed,": signals that confirming evidence follows, so it fits — the warbler example proves that cascades can be harmful. ✓
  • Choice D — "In addition,": signals a new parallel point, so it fails because the study does not add a new claim — it supports the one just made. ✘
That matches choice C — C is correct.
